revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=加須市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[加須市]][[南大桑]]3478 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 14.7 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 38 |経度秒 = 37.7 |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=加須市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[加須市]][[南大桑]]3478 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 14.7 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 38 |経度秒 = 37.7 |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[加須市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。口伝によれば、元々は旧家の門井家の屋敷の北に隣接していたが、屋敷の拡大に伴い、丘の上に移設したという。近くの[[定泰寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年、922-923p</ref>。
[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の3社が合祀された。[[1909年]]（明治42年）に神明社とその境内社を合祀した<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *南大桑の獅子舞（加須市指定無形民俗文化財 昭和35年9月8日指定）<ref name="市">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html 加須地域指定文化財一覧]加須市</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *[[花崎駅]]より徒歩25分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:加須市の歴史]] [[Category:加須市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:雷電神社]]

According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old l..."
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Movement|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kazo, Saitama]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[ja:雷電神社]]

In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.comment: adding defaultsort to all of them
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|12=draft}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=draft}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, Raiden Shrine was ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy|12=draft}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|12=draft}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo|ja|加須市|12=draft}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanazaki Station|ja|花崎駅|12=draft}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=加須市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[加須市]][[南大桑]]3478 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 14.7 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 38 |経度秒 = 37.7 |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[加須市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。口伝によれば、元々は旧家の門井家の屋敷の北に隣接していたが、屋敷の拡大に伴い、丘の上に移設したという。近くの[[定泰寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年、922-923p</ref>。
[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の3社が合祀された。[[1909年]]（明治42年）に神明社とその境内社を合祀した<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *南大桑の獅子舞（加須市指定無形民俗文化財 昭和35年9月8日指定）<ref name="市">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html 加須地域指定文化財一覧]加須市</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *[[花崎駅]]より徒歩25分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:雷電神社]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kazo|qid=Q851680}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.12075|N|139.643806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131900994]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōtai-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Raiden Shrine was Ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]], three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo]]. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|Hanazaki Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama}} {{Shinto-stub}}

It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōtai-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Raiden Shrine was Ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]], three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo]]. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|Hanazaki Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.12075|N|139.643806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131900994]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōtai-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Raiden Shrine was Ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]], three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo]]. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|Hanazaki Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.12075|N|139.643806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131900994]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} and is classified as a former [[Son-sha]] under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōtai-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Raiden Shrine was Ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]], three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo]]. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|Hanazaki Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to oral tradition, the shrine was originally located to the north of the estate of the Kadoi family, an old local family. As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōtai-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Raiden Shrine was Ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]], three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo]]. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|Hanazaki Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.

As the estate expanded, the shrine was relocated to a nearby hilltop. The neighboring {{ill|Jōtai-ji|ja|定泰寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōtai-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (managing temple).<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 922–923.</ref>
In 1872, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, Raiden Shrine was Ranked as a "Village Shrine" (村社). During the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, three surrounding shrines were merged into Raiden Shrine in 1908. In 1909, the shrine also absorbed {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} and its associated shrines.<ref name="jinja"></ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Lion Dance of Minami-Ōkuwa''' (南大桑の獅子舞) – This traditional lion dance is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo}}. It was officially recognized on September 8, 1960.<ref name="city">[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kazochiiki/6497.html Kazo Area Designated Cultural Properties], Kazo City.</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|en|Hanasaki Station (Saitama)|WD=Q5646939|lt=Hanazaki Station}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, Kita Saitama, Chichibu,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1986.
